debug_info.goal:
U V : Type
⊢ U ∼ V ↔ ∃ f g, f ∘ g = id ∧ g ∘ f = id

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
by
  constructor
  · -- Prove the forward direction: if U ∼ V, then there exist f and g with the required properties
    intro h
    have h₁ : Nonempty (U ≃ V) := h
    obtain ⟨e⟩ := h₁
    refine' ⟨e, e.symm, _⟩
    constructor
    · -- Prove that e ∘ e.symm = id
      funext x
      simp [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
    · -- Prove that e.symm ∘ e = id
      funext x
      simp [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
  · -- Prove the backward direction: if such f and g exist, then U ∼ V
    rintro ⟨f, g, hf, hg⟩
    -- We use the fact that f and g are inverses to construct an equivalence
    have h₁ : Function.Bijective f := by
      constructor
      · -- Prove that f is injective
        intro a b h
        have h₂ := congr_fun hg a
        have h₃ := congr_fun hg b
        simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₂ h₃
        have h₄ := congr_fun hf (g a)
        have h₅ := congr_fun hf (g b)
        simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₄ h₅
        simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        <;> aesop
      · -- Prove that f is surjective
        intro b
        have h₂ := congr_fun hf (g b)
        simp [Function.comp_apply] at h₂
        have h₃ : f (g b) = b := by
          simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
        exact ⟨g b, by simp [h₃]⟩
    -- Since f is bijective, we can construct an equivalence
    have h₂ : Nonempty (U ≃ V) := by
      refine' ⟨Equiv.ofBijective f h₁⟩
    exact h₂
